pages.raw_text:
The topic before the congress
this morning was irrigation by
Private Enterprise"


F. c. Goudy of Denver, former
president of the congress, and
George E. Barstow, of Texas, also
former president of the congress,
were the principal speakers.


Goudy opened by describing the
growth of irrigation in the west
from the small rude bitches of the
farmer to the time when the gov
eminent began lending its aid to
the agriculturist. He described the
formation of irrigation corpora
tions, particularly in Colorado; of
the work of those companies,
through designing organizers,
through lack OF able agents, and
holly scored the water pirates"
who file water claims to prevent
bonafide enterprises from using
streams except at exorbitant prices.
